content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
Catherine T. Redmond 
District Counsel 
Central Region (Main Office) 
1990 E. Gettysburg A venue 
Fresno, CA 93 726 ·0244 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
November 28, 2012 
Re: 
Your Request for Informal Assistance 
Our File No. 1-12-162 
Dear Ms. Redmond: 
This letter responds to your request for advice on behalf of Leroy Ornellas, Supervisor 
for San Joauin County, 1 regarding the revolving door provisions of the Political Reform Act 
(the "Act").- Because you have not provided any facts related to a specific appearance before or 
communication to the government agency employers that Supervisor Ornellas will soon be 
leaving, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.3 
QUESTIONS 
1. Will the one-year ban under Section 87406.3 apply to Supervisor Ornellas, who was 
appointed, not elected, to the District's Governing Board? 
2. Does the one-year ban under Section 87406. l prohibit a former District Governing 
Board member from representing clients before District staff in permitting and enforcement 
matters? 
1 Supervisor Ornellas also serves as a member of 1he Governing Board for 1he San Joaquin Valley Unified 
Air Pollulion Conlrol Oislrict ( .. Dis1ricf'). 
2 The Political Reform Act is conlained in Government Code Sec1ions 81000 through 91014. Al I slalutory 
references are lo lhe Government Code, unless olherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission are contained in Seclions 18110 through 18997 of Tille 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All 
regulatory references are to Title 2. Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated. 
·
1 Informal assistance does not provide 1he requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Seclion 83114: Regulation I 8329(c).) 


CONCLUSIONS 
File No. 1-12-162 
Page No. 2 
1. Yes, but the prohibitions under Section 87406.3 will only apply to any appearances 
before or communications to the San Joaquin County Board of Supervisors. With respect to any 
appearances before or communications to the District Governing Board, only the provisions 
under Section 87406. l will apply to prohibit him from influencing any regulatory action. 
2. No, the provisions under Section 87406. l prohibit a former District Governing Board 
member from making any appearances before or communications to the District Governing 
Board only for the purpose of influencing regulatory action. Because "regulatory action" does 
not encompass permitting and enforcement matters, such activity is permissible. 
FACTS 
Supervisor Ornellas was appointed to the District's Governing Board by his fellow 
Supervisors in 2007. At the end of 2012, Supervisor Ornellas will be leaving both his positions 
as a Supervisor for San Joaquin County and a member of the District's Governing Board. He 
subsequently intends to pursue a private consulting career that will involve interaction with the 
District on behalf of clients for such matters as submitting permit applications to District staff, 
meeting and/or negotiating with District staff on permitting issues, participating in variance 
hearings and permit appeals before the District hearing board and negotiating with District staff 
on enforcement matters. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act contains two revolving door provisions (Sections 87406.1 and 87406.3) that 
address specified one-year prohibitions for local governmental officials who leave governmental 
service. Section 87406.3 applies to elected officials and chief administrative officers or general 
managers of a county, city, or special district and generally prohibits them from appearing before 
or communicating with their former agency for the purpose of influencing any legislative or 
administrative actions, including quasi-legislative and quasi-judicial actions. Section 87406. l 
applies to former members of air pollution control district boards, among others, and generally 
prohibits them from appearing before or communicating with their former agency for the 
purpose of influencing regulatory action.4 
The facts provided in your letter indicate that Supervisor Ornellas will be leaving his 
positions as a county supervisor and a member of the District's Governing Board at the end of 
2012. As an elected official and member of the District's Governing Board, Supervisor Ornellas 
will thus be subject to both of the post-governmental employment prohibitions under Sections 
87406.1 and 87406.3. However, for a former local elected official, Section 87406.3 applies only 
4 An additional revolving door restriction, Section 87407, also applies until lhat date on which Supervisor 
Ornellas leaves office. Under Section 87407, Supervisor Ornellas is prohibited from making. participating in 
making, or using his official position to influence a governmental decision if the decision directly relates to a 
prospective employer. (See Regulation 18747.) 


File No. 1-12-162 
Page No. 3 
to appearances before or communication with the agency to which the fonner official had 
previously been elected, or an agency whose budget, personnel, and other operations or subject 
to the direction and control of the agency to which the fonner official had previously been 
elected. (Regulation 18746.3(b)(6).) For purposes of any appearances with or communications 
to the District, Supervisor Ornellas will be limited only by the provisions under Section 
87406.1.5 Thus, for one year subsequent to leaving his position as a member of the District's 
Governing Board, he will be banned from appearing before or communicating with them for the 
purpose of influencing regulatory action. 
As stated in the Jay Advice Letter, which you reference in your letter, the term 
"regulatory action" is not defined in Section 87406.1. (Jay Advice Letter, No. 1-11-055.) 
However, it has been detennined that "regulatory action" should be interpreted broadly to 
include every rule, regulation, or other action in any rate-making proceeding or any quasi­
legislative proceeding before an air pollution control district or an air quality control district. 
(Ibid., see also Wood Advice Letters, Nos. 1-95-167 and I-95-232.) 
Accordingly, the one-year ban under Section 87406.1 would not prohibit Supervisor 
Ornellas from influencing District proceedings other than a "regulatory action." As applied to 
your facts, this would therefore pennit him to make appearances before or communications to 
District staff on behalf of clients on pennitting or enforcement matters such as submitting permit 
applications to District staff, meeting and/or negotiating with District staff on permitting issues, 
participating in variance hearings and permit appeals before the District hearing board and 
negotiating with District staff on enforcement matters.6 
JW:jgl 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
By: 
Sincerely. 
Zackery P. Morazzini 
General Counsel 
 
Sen or Counsel, Legal Division 
5 Supervisor Ornellas will also be subject to the one-year ban pursuant to Section 87406.3, but only with 
respect to any appearances before or communications with the San Joaquin County Board of Supervisors. Because 
your letter does not seek advice concerning any such appearances, we do not analyze that issue further. 
6 Of course, the other activities mentioned in your letter such as "assisting in preparing permit applications 
for clients, which the clients then submit to District staff. advising clients on the appropriate District staff member to 
contact on a given issue and advising clients on enforcement matters without directly contacting District staff' 
would likewise be permitted under Section 87406.1.

JW:jgl
